Defensive copy of caller-supplied read-only collection allocates unconditionally; prefer type-check + cast to mutable interface before falling back to copy

Two-layer outer/inner try-catch pattern repeated across DispatchSafelyAsync(Action) and OpenRecoveryDialogAsync(Func<Task>) within the same service - panel failed to apply catalog rule code-duplication-dry whose discovery signature explicitly calls out 'same try/catch/log block repeated across services'; REPEAT panel-execution failure of an already-catalogued rule (second occurrence in this session - first was an earlier-session TryOpenModalAsync helper)

Agent ran git commit --amend + git push --force-with-lease without emitting PRE-COMMIT GATE PASSED block in same turn - bypassed mandatory user diff-approval gate per pre-commit.md/panel-policy.md §User diff-approval. Triggering condition: PR-comment-driven amendment treated as routine rather than as a new commit-producing event requiring its own diff approval. User called the violation out post-push.

Copilot reviewer flagged readonly+out field as compile error inside class ctor - false positive: C# permits assignment via out inside declaring class instance ctor (ECMA-334 §15.5.3). Catalog hardened with FP-3 entry

Agent went straight from pre-impl panel READY (4-slot R4.2 unanimous) to git commit + git push without running the post-implementation multi-model review (post-code-change.md step 3) or emitting the POST-CODE-CHANGE LEDGER block; user prompted "you skipped the pre commit review" after push; a retroactive single-agent code-review caught one MEDIUM asymmetry (callback exception handling missing in RemoveDatabaseAsync that was present in ResolveImportConflictsAsync) that the design panel could not see because it reviews specs not implementations. THIRD occurrence of pre-commit-gate bypass in this series (after pre-commit-gate-bypass-on-amend in an earlier PR and another silent skip earlier).

Interface XML summary stayed narrow after the interface was extended with new members (ILogReloadCoordinator gained HasActiveLogs + ReloadAllActiveLogsAsync; summary still described only database-removal orchestration). Panel (4 reviewers + 11-cat prompt incl. category 5 Documentation accuracy) missed it; Copilot reviewer flagged it on first push. Pattern: interface-extension XML doc staleness.

Extracted helper method inherited a comment that described the ORIGINAL site's adjacent operations (_disposed check + dispatcher call) but the extracted body has only the dispatcher call. Panel (post-impl + §2D) missed it across two rounds; Copilot caught it in PR round 4. Pattern: extraction-comment-drift.

Copilot PR review flagged RemoveDatabaseAsync entry-CT-check missing (round 5); same pattern shape also present at ImportAsync + ApplyPendingTogglesAsync (sister sites) but our panel + reviewer caught only the bot-cited site initially. Self-similarity sweep on the fix round was mandated only after the user pushed back. Pattern: bot flagged 1 instance of a recurring shape; sister sites stayed unfixed across multiple amend rounds because no explicit self-similarity sweep happens in the bot-fix workflow. THIRD recurrence of incomplete-sweep failure in this PR (round 2: UpgradeDatabaseAsync; round 5: ImportAsync + ApplyPendingTogglesAsync).

confirmRemoveAsync declared as nullable optional (Func<...>? = null) with null-default semantic = silent refusal (return NotConfirmed without invoking RemoveAsync). Asymmetric with askOverwriteAsync (null → skip-conflicts → still proceeds). Panel (4-slot pre-impl + 4-slot post-impl + 4-slot §2D mirror) all missed despite category 10 in the 11-cat prompt covering ambiguous boolean params + magic-number defaults. Bot caught it round 6. Fix: made confirmRemoveAsync required (drop = null + add ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull at entry). Pattern catalog updated with explicit slug.

XML summary fix applied in round 3 (commit 2a18ecce) was silently LOST during subsequent amend chain; user pushback in round 5 led to re-adding it; then it was lost AGAIN in round 6 commit 3a8ddd8d before a focused code-review caught it. Pattern: working-tree edit makes it into the agent diff view but does not make it into the next git commit object. THREE recurrences in this PR (rounds 3-4 transition + rounds 5-6 transition + round 6 self-similarity sweep regression). Likely cause: edit tool returns success but file write does not persist OR git add silently fails for some path. Mitigation added: pre-commit.md hard gate now requires staged_diff_verified field cross-checking git diff --cached against the shown-diff before the commit can proceed.

AddEventLogRuntime DI registration extension method has XML <summary> documenting required external registrations (AddFluxor, AddEventLogFiltering, AddEventLogProviderDatabase) but the doc was not updated when an earlier PR added DatabaseOperationCoordinator singleton (which depends on host-provided IFilePickerService). Downstream hosts get DI activation failure at runtime when Settings opens. Panel (4-slot pre-impl + 4-slot post-impl + 4-slot §2D mirror + 2 single-agent code-reviews) all missed despite a previously-strengthened doc-impl-mismatch interface-extension sub-case. Bot caught it round 7. Pattern: stale DI-registration-method contract doc after adding a new singleton with host-provided dependency.

BuildImportSummary emits StringBuilder.AppendLine + bullet glyphs (multi-line format) for failure detail. Original code routed to AlertDialogService.ShowAlert (CSS white-space: pre-wrap → newlines preserved). An earlier PR re-routed to IInfoBannerService.ReportInfoBanner / IErrorBannerService.ReportError. Banner CSS (banner.css:76) is white-space: nowrap + text-overflow: ellipsis → newlines collapse to spaces; multi-failure detail becomes unreadable. Panel + multiple bot rounds all missed; bot caught round 8. Pattern: output formatter built for renderer A re-routed to renderer B without re-validating renderer B can preserve the format. Fix applied: single-line FormatFailureSummary using comma separators; matches banner's single-line CSS constraint.

SettingsModal.AskOverwriteAsync (UI callback impl) had `catch (OperationCanceledException) { return false; }` swallowing OCE and converting cancellation to Skip-this-file. The coordinator side (DatabaseOperationCoordinator.ResolveImportConflictsAsync) explicitly does `catch (OperationCanceledException) { throw; }` to let OCE bubble up to RunOperationAsync<T> which returns ImportOutcome.None on OCE (silent abort). The UI swallow broke the cross-component contract: instead of aborting the import on cancellation the conflict-resolution loop kept iterating and the import proceeded with skipped conflicts. Panel (4-slot pre-impl + 4-slot post-impl + 4-slot §2D mirror + multiple bot rounds) all missed. Bot caught round 9. Sibling instance: RemoveDatabase inline callback at SettingsModal.razor.cs:267 had the SAME pattern (self-similarity sweep caught it). Fix: removed both `catch (OperationCanceledException)` swallows. Added Runtime test `ImportAsync_OverwriteCallbackThrowsOperationCanceled_AbortsImportNoBannerAndReturnsNone` to lock the contract. Pattern catalog updated with new slug.

DatabaseCoordinationEffects.ReloadAllActiveLogsAsync was named 'reload' (clear sequential implication of close-then-reopen) and returned Task but its body did fire-and-forget Dispatch(CloseAllLogsAction) immediately followed by Dispatch(OpenLog) per log returning Task.CompletedTask. The CloseAllLogsAction effect handler runs heavyweight async work (CancelAllLoads CancelAllReloads RemoveAllAsync cache clears) concurrently with the new OpenLog actions causing race conditions where CancelAllLoads cancels the just-dispatched OpenLog loads and RemoveAllAsync removes watchers that the new opens just registered. Sibling PrepareForDatabaseRemovalAsync had the CORRECT pattern (per-log RegisterCloseCompletion + AcquireCoordinatorLock + per-log CloseLogAction + await with timeout) yet ReloadAllActiveLogsAsync was implemented as a shortcut. Pre-impl panel + post-impl panel + multiple bot rounds missed; bot caught round 10. Fix applied: re-implemented ReloadAllActiveLogsAsync using the per-log TCS-await pattern. Updated XML doc to accurately describe sequential semantics with timeout. Added 3 new intentful tests (sequential ordering NOT bypass-able when close completion fails; lock-release on success path; lock-release on failure path).

ReloadAllActiveLogsAsync (and sibling PrepareForDatabaseRemovalAsync) registers per-log TaskCompletionSource entries before entering the wait loop. When one log times out the catch removes only the failing entry via RemoveStrandedCompletion(id); registered entries for other logs leak in the close coordinator dictionary and can interfere with subsequent coordination. Bot caught round 11. Fix applied to both methods: catch loops over all waiters and removes every stranded completion before rethrowing.

ApplyPendingTogglesAsync used 'if (ct.IsCancellationRequested) return;' inside the toggle loop. Mid-iteration cancellation silently returned after applying a prefix of toggles; caller awaiting the Task could not distinguish 'all complete' from 'cancelled after N of M applied'. Bot caught round 11. Fix applied: replaced early-return with ThrowIfCancellationRequested so OCE propagates and caller observes the cancellation. Added regression test verifying partial-prefix application does not happen silently.

PromptAndReloadOpenLogs in SettingsModal awaited ReloadAllActiveLogsAsync without try/catch. After round-10 made ReloadAllActiveLogsAsync genuinely sequential (and thus throwable), a TimeoutException or OCE from the close phase would propagate up and break the modal-close flow. Bot caught round 11. Fix applied: wrapped the await in try/catch handling OperationCanceledException and TimeoutException; both treated as soft-failures so Settings close remains robust.

DatabaseOperationCoordinator RunOperationAsync wrappers logged exceptions as _logger.Warning($"...: {ex.Message}"). ex.Message drops stack and InnerException making field diagnostics impossible. 4 affected log call sites in coordinator. Banner messages (user-facing) correctly continue to use ex.Message; only diagnostic logger sinks need full ex. Bot caught round 12. Fix: replaced ex.Message with ex in all 4 _logger.Warning sites (kept ex.Message in _errorBanners.ReportError user-facing messages).

ReloadAllActiveLogsAsync round-10/11 fix threw immediately on close failure without attempting best-effort reopen. After dispatching CloseLogAction for all snapshotted logs the close phase could fail mid-way; rethrowing without reopen left the UI in 'all closed none reopened' state. Bot caught round 12. Fix: deferred the throw via a local Exception variable and always run the OpenLog reopen loop after the close-await loop; throw the deferred close failure AFTER the best-effort reopen attempt. Reopen uses CancellationToken.None so a cancelled token does not skip the restoration. Extended partial-cleanup-on-async-failure-path slug to cover state-restoration requirement.

SettingsModal PromptAndReloadOpenLogs round-11 fix swallowed OperationCanceledException AND TimeoutException silently with no logging. OCE swallow is acceptable (cancellation expected during teardown) but Timeout represents a real failure (close pipeline hung 30s) and operators investigating 'reload silently did nothing' had no diagnostic trail. Bot caught round 12. Fix: split catch into OperationCanceledException (silent swallow) and TimeoutException (Log via newly-injected ITraceLogger as TraceLogger property with full ex). Extended await-throwable-async-call-without-handler-in-teardown-flow slug to require at-minimum-log when swallowing non-OCE.

ReloadAllActiveLogsAsync round-12 deferred-throw pattern stored exception in Exception? deferredCloseFailure local + rethrew via throw deferredCloseFailure. CLR semantics: throw <variable> resets Exception.StackTrace to the rethrow site losing the original throw point (the WaitAsync timeout). Operators see the rethrow line not the actual fault line. Bot caught round 13. Fix applied: changed local to ExceptionDispatchInfo? and used ExceptionDispatchInfo.Capture(ex) + deferredEx?.Throw() per the reference pattern in FilterService.cs. Preserves original stack trace through deferred rethrow.

PrepareForDatabaseRemovalAsync dispatched CloseLogAction for all active logs upfront (parallel dispatch) and added each log to the caller-owned LogReopenSnapshot only AFTER its WaitAsync succeeded. On the first close timeout/cancel the method threw - but CloseLogAction had already reduced each log out of ActiveLogs. Logs whose dispatched-close completion was still in-flight (or already completed but not yet awaited) ended up closed yet missing from the snapshot. The caller (RemoveDatabaseAsync) reopens snapshot.Items only - so those mid-flight logs were silently dropped. The previous round-12 catalog enhancement explicitly listed PrepareForDatabaseRemovalAsync as an acceptable case (caller-snapshot exists) which was false comfort. Bot caught round 14. Fix applied: catch block adds ALL waiters (deduped via HashSet) to the snapshot before rethrowing so the caller's reopen covers everything that was dispatched. Catalog acceptable-clause tightened to require synchronous snapshot population per iteration OR catch-block snapshot expansion on failure.

BuildImportSummary composed user-facing banner text by concatenating the lowercase fragment 'failed: db.db (reason)' (produced by FormatFailureSummary) after either '; ' (semicolon - grammatical) or '. ' (period - UNGRAMMATICAL because the next sentence should start capitalized). The zero-imported case produced 'No databases were imported. failed: ...' which reads as a broken sentence to end users. Bot caught round 15. Fix applied: changed the zero-imported case from period to semicolon: 'No databases were imported; failed: ...' which is grammatical (semicolon connects two independent clauses without case-change). Added focused test BuildImportSummary_AllFailedZeroImported_MessageJoinsWithSemicolonNotPeriodToPreserveLowercaseFragment locking the new contract. Catalog gets new slug user-facing-text-fragment-case-mismatch-on-concatenation.

ResolveImportConflictsAsync outer foreach over sourcePaths and inner foreach over candidateNames had no per-iteration cancellation token checks. Cancellation was observed only when (a) the path was a zip (awaiting EnumerateZipDbEntryNamesAsync) or (b) askOverwriteAsync was non-null and the callback itself honored CT. For non-zip paths with askOverwriteAsync=null the method iterated to completion regardless of cancellation. Entry-CT-throw was present (round 2 fix) but was insufficient by itself for long-running loops. Cancellation-handling has been an ongoing hotspot (rounds 2 entry-throw + 5 self-similarity sweep + 6 PrepareForDatabaseRemovalAsync entry + 11 ApplyPendingToggles silent-return + 16 missing-loop-CT-check) - 5 rounds before this pattern was added to the catalog. Bot caught round 16. Fix applied: added cancellationToken.ThrowIfCancellationRequested at the start of each loop iteration. New slug async-loop-no-intermediate-cancellation-check (HIGH tier) covers the gap.

Meta-process violation: when bot findings revealed missing-gap catalog rules across earlier-PR rounds 6 8 9 10 11 12 13 15 16, the agent added/enhanced the rule but did NOT trigger a full-scan of the active diff against the new rule. Consequence: the same PR carried multiple instances of the new pattern that the bot surfaced one-by-one across subsequent rounds (cancellation-handling alone took 5 rounds; partial-cleanup-on-async-failure took 3 rounds). User explicitly called out 'we have done 2-3 amends related to this' in round 16. Fix applied: new HIGH-tier slug full-scan-against-new-rule-not-triggered-after-bot-finding (catalog) + new HARD GATE AGENTS.md sect 1A.3 + new full_scan_results required field in PRE-COMMIT GATE PASSED block + pre-commit.md playbook updated with field definition. Process now: catalog edit triggers immediate full-scan of new rule's audit-method against entire PR diff with gap inventory in the gate block.

Agent transitioned from Phase 1 implementation (bUnit test passing, lint clean, PR-quality-gate ack block emitted) directly to git add + git commit + git push --force-with-lease to a new origin branch without ANY ask_user invocation. Bypassed §0 git add, git commit, and git push gates simultaneously. User pushback verbatim: 'never gave approval to git add or git commit, why are we doing this again, please fix the instructions'. THIRD occurrence of pre-commit-gate bypass in the SoC series (an earlier PR amend, an earlier PR post-impl-skip, an earlier PR fresh-impl). Root cause: agent's mental model treats PR-quality-gate ack block emission as sufficient gate-passage; does not distinguish catalog (mechanical) gate from §0 (human) gate. Fix applied: AGENTS.md READ FIRST line now explicitly cites §0; AGENTS.md §0 gains dual-gate distinction callout + PRE-GIT SENTINEL phase-transition checkpoint + new git push (non-force) sub-section covering first push of a new branch; new HIGH-tier catalog slug implementation-to-commit-transition-bypasses-user-approval (now 24 HIGH-tier slugs, must be acknowledged on every commit going forward).

Post-Code-Change LEDGER claimed '73 new comment lines all WCAG/contract/test-rationale/design-rationale (no vestigial comments)' as a summary assertion, then user rejected diff approval with 'comment viloations' (typo for violations). Investigation found ~26 narrative comments violating §3.1 'Only comment code that needs a bit of clarification': test-narrative blocks restating what tests assert (10 across ToggleTests/OptionSelectTests/ValueSelectTests/TextInputTests/FilterRowChromeTests/DatabaseEntryRowTests), design-rationale duplicated across CSS files (Slot 3 X+ plan / segmented-control narrative / AAA-vs-AA discussion), user-elected-deferred commit-1/commit-2 jargon in DatabaseToolsTab XML doc, EffectiveAriaLabel XML doc duplicating its one-line body, stale Razor @* comment in DatabaseToolsModal referencing 'dropdown' (control was renamed OptionSelect). Fix applied: condensed/removed comments; diff went 73167 → 65769 bytes (-10.1%). Comment-audit cleanup gated by focused rubber-duck R3 pass on comment-only delta. Distinct from per-comment-justification requirement: gate block needs to ENUMERATE each new comment with one-line defense rather than summary claim 'all comments are defensible'.

HARD GATE WORKING-AS-DESIGNED: staged_diff_verified cross-check (introduced after an earlier PR round 6 lost-fix incident) CAUGHT a real divergence between the diff the user approved (sha256 5C494C0D) and the staged content at commit-time (sha256 A8BC019E). 179-byte divergence: user inline-edited InputComponent.cs Values property 'default!' → 'null!' during the approval window (between agent capturing shown-diff at 13:21 and capturing staged-diff at 13:31). Agent surfaced the delta to user via ask_user before committing rather than committing silently. User confirmed include (semantic-equivalent reference-type initialization idiom). Outcome: committed at 1ecdb599 with explicit documented_delta in PRE-COMMIT GATE PASSED block. No catalog change needed - this is documentation that the existing HARD GATE caught a real (user-introduced) divergence and the agent honored the gate instead of bypassing.

NSubstitute test helper made standalone _menuService.OpenAt(Arg.Any...) call outside a When/Returns/Do context - recorded as real invocation polluting Received() call history. Bot iter 1 fix: delete the standalone call; WhenForAnyArgs(s => s.OpenAt(default, ...)).Do(callback) is sufficient setup.

JS interop attach/detach asymmetry - OnAfterRenderAsync gated attach on tablist-rendered state and reset _tabKeyShimAttached=false on state-flip but never called JS detach. WeakMap-shim self-cleans via GC but contract was asymmetric. Bot iter 2 fix: add explicit detach call before flag reset with same JSDisconnectedException/JSException swallowing as attach branch.

Multi-rung focus cascade preemptively cleared _pendingFocusToActiveTab=false BEFORE attempting tab-button SafelyAsync - if tab ref missing or SafelyAsync swallowed exception, no active-tab fallback fired in same render. §2D Round 2 Slot 5 verified ROW-focus cascade but missed TAB-focus cascade. Bot iter 3 fix: use TrySafelyAsync returning bool, set fallback flag to !focused, restructure else-if as independent if (!focused && ...) so cascade chains in same render.

Positional record AddToPresetIntent override Filter property with { get; } (no init) - applying record-non-nullable-ctor-validation fix preserved validation but shadowed auto-generated init setter, breaking with { Filter = ... } at compile time. Symmetric concern with existing record-non-nullable-ctor-validation slug. Bot iter 4 fix: add init; to property override ({ get; init; } retains both validation on construction AND with-mutation support).

Test comment block claimed verification via reflection-based count and NSubstitute subscriber-count introspection - but the actual oracle was simpler (raise event after Dispose; no-exception = unsubscribed via bUnit's disposed-renderer guard). 7 lines of misleading comments. Sub-case of code-comment-mismatch specific to test patterns. Bot iter 5 fix: pure deletion per §3.1 deletion-exempt; test name + Raise.Event body are self-documenting.
aria-controls referenced an element id rendered only inside a conditional block; reference dangles when the target is collapsed or absent
combobox aria-expanded bound to a state flag while the listbox renders only when suggestions exist; advertises expanded=true with no popup in the DOM
a combobox input was a direct child of a role=list container alongside chip listitems; invalid ARIA list structure
programmatic smooth scrollIntoView with no prefers-reduced-motion fallback
an icon glyph class referenced did not exist in the pinned icon-font version and rendered blank
create-path name-collision validation checked all entries cross-kind while the rename-path checked same-kind only; divergent invariant for one concept
a validation-error background used a hard-coded rgba literal instead of the existing semantic color token; wrong in dark mode
an enum-stringification test asserted on nameof(member) instead of the runtime ToString() the component actually emits
a source-scanning invariant test matched only the bare attribute form and missed the @-prefixed expression form, letting violating sites pass
multiple new text inputs lacked an aria-label (label absence, not polarity); catalog aria coverage existed but the panel did not apply it per-control across a large diff
tag usage counts recomputed per-tag by re-scanning all entries on every render (O(n*m)); performance rule present in base instructions but not applied
tab next/prev index advance lacked an explicit lower-bound guard; high-frequency catalog bounds rule existed but the panel did not apply it
a dropdown row label reimplemented an existing label-formatting helper inline; DRY-remediation HARD GATE in base instructions but the panel did not apply it

circuit-breaker 'delete unloadable rows unless systemic' used totalRows (incl. kept unknown-kind forward-version rows) as the ratio denominator; out-of-domain rows diluted the ratio and suppressed the guard, allowing deletion of unloadable known-kind rows in a forward-version scenario

per-element loop-invariant collection-building call recomputed inside a LINQ predicate lambda (Outer(e, Inner(set))) giving O(N*M); the existing n-squared-selection-scan catalog query matched only foreach-over-selected, so the LINQ-lambda variant slipped the preflight

a new public interface member shipped with no XML doc while its sibling members were documented; the doc-impl-mismatch pattern checked existing docs for accuracy but had no check for missing docs on new public surface

a reconcile/prune sweep over a dictionary of component refs removed entries only when the key left the live set, missing entries whose stored value was nulled while the key stayed live, so dangling entries accumulated across the session

Copilot reviewer flagged 5 instruction-doc refs using a wrong .github subdirectory prefix (e.g. playbooks/X.md missing .github/) or a bare workflow X.yml; fixed inline. A broad review-pass slug was drafted but DROPPED as over-broad (it would false-positive on the established bare-leaf playbook/policy/script reference convention used 40+ times across the corpus); proper fix is a check-playbook-refs.ps1 enhancement to detect wrong-prefix/unresolvable refs (machinery, deferred to Batch E)

Copilot reviewer flagged a non-runnable inline PowerShell snippet in the maintenance playbook (-replace CRLF,LF used CRLF/LF as bare tokens instead of escape sequences); fixed. Reveals the existing doc-impl-mismatch slug does not explicitly cover runnable code/command examples in instruction docs; proposed fix = extend doc-impl-mismatch (catalog-ext, deferred to Batch E)

Copilot reviewer flagged the new read-receipt-token comment-audit exemption as applying to ALL file types (matched by content marker only), a bypass vector in .razor/.cshtml/.html/.xml where <!-- is also a comment token; scoped the exemption to markdown-only + added a regression test asserting non-md <!-- comments stay flagged. General lesson: a coverage/security check exemption must be guarded by the file-type/context allowlist where the marker is actually emitted (proposed slug deferred to a Batch E panel)

Follow-up review round flagged the same read-receipt-token comment-audit exemption as under-anchored: the regex matched any line starting with the marker, so an unclosed opener or trailing text after the closer could still bypass the comment-audit count. Tightened to require the full closed single-line form (exactly an 8-hex token plus the --> closer, end-anchored) + added regression tests for the malformed variants. General lesson sibling to the context-scope miss: a coverage/security-check exemption pattern must be fully anchored (require the terminator, reject trailing content), not just prefix-matched (proposed slug deferred to a Batch E panel)

Copilot reviewer on the de-projectify PR flagged residual internal inconsistencies the token-rename pass left: a renamed identifier (WidgetNames) next to un-renamed sibling terms (XLog* in the same example), stale domain-noun prose (active databases) around a renamed interface, and in-this-repo phrasing implying a placeholder solution file exists. Lesson for de-projectify/maintenance: when genericizing an identifier, also genericize sibling terms in the SAME example + surrounding prose nouns + strip existence-implying phrasing; a future check-project-refs enhancement could diff renamed-token examples for sibling-term drift

Round-2 recurrence of the de-projectify-hygiene class: genericizing a project ref left content that only made sense for the original specific (README validation step kept which had ~46 findings + 7 FPs after the PR number was generalized - now orphaned/non-actionable) and an example that no longer matched the canonical format used elsewhere in the same doc (pre-pr-push SSH greeting user@github.com vs the Hi <username>! format in the SSH-verification row above). Reinforces the deferred check-project-refs enhancement: after a de-projectify rename, re-read the surrounding sentence for orphaned specifics + cross-check sibling/canonical formats in the same file

Round-3 recurrence of the de-projectify-hygiene class: (a) a commit-message BAD example still said lazy tags after its sibling rows were genericized to CategoryDisplayName (sibling-term drift, same as rounds 1-2); (b) NEW sub-mode - after claiming in the PR/playbook that the consuming-pr-N token survives only in the data ledger, two shipped-prose spots (panel-policy -PrRef schema example consuming-pr-<N> and the maintenance-playbook csv-exception bullet) still printed the token, contradicting the confinement claim. Lesson: a confinement/scope CLAIM about an identifier must itself be grep-verified (the claim is falsified if the token appears anywhere the claim excludes); and genericized prose must not re-print the very token it says is data-only. Reinforces the deferred check-project-refs enhancement
A script uses the PS6+ automatic variable $IsWindows for an OS check without a PS-version short-circuit, so it throws under StrictMode on Windows PowerShell 5.1
A CI step suppresses git stderr (2>$null) and does not check $LASTEXITCODE, so a failed fetch/rev-list leaves the commit list empty and the hard-gate job passes without running the checker
A delimited registry/config parser does not detect duplicate key rows, so a duplicate silently overrides the intended wiring instead of failing loud
A cross-store parity check compares only the keys and not the mapped values, so an entry wired to the wrong target still passes
A code comment references a sibling script/file that does not exist in this repo/branch, making the rationale unverifiable for maintainers

Agent committed a non-compliant commit message (Co-authored-by trailer, conflicting with pre-commit.md) during an earlier consuming PR; only an agent self-report guarded it, so a fresh session would repeat it.
divergence_override_history: in-session correction during an earlier consuming PR

finding_brief: Agent missed CI-only checks (generated catalog not regenerated, README 30KB cap) before pushing an earlier PR, costing CI rounds; no local mirror enumerated/ran every workflow check pre-push.
divergence_override_history: in-session correction during an earlier consuming PR

finding_brief: Agent ran git add -A this session, auto-staging code instead of letting the user stage as the review signal; the old AGENTS.md model had the agent drive staging, so a fresh agent would repeat it.
divergence_override_history: model was agent-stages until this change

finding_brief: Diff-review panel caught 5 stale consumers a model-inversion (git-add gate) sweep omitted: .github/pr-quality-gate/README.md (pre_git_add_user_approval field), playbooks/manifest.yaml (stage only touched files), the PRE-GIT SENTINEL template + catalog slug step-3 (user_diff_approval:pending not the scope-marker), and review-workflow-gates.md overclaim. A semantic-rename sweep must enumerate secondary surfaces: README/design-doc schemas, manifest.yaml descriptions, catalog slug step-text, sentinel/ledger TEMPLATE field values, and all mechanical-guarantee phrasing variants - not just the obvious rule files.
divergence_override_history: primary files swept; secondary surfaces missed

finding_brief: check-commit-message.ps1 -MessageFile mode (new) lacked the blank-second-line no-body test that range-mode already had; a wrapper .Trim(LF) pre-stripped trailing newlines so 'Subject\n\n' wrongly PASSED strict no-body. When adding a second mode/path to a checker, mirror the existing mode's edge-case tests onto the new path.

Copilot bot caught a coverage-gate bypass the full panel missed: run-local-ci.ps1 Test-ScriptInvocation matched a scripts/...ps1 path ANYWHERE in a command (regex (?:^|\s)), so 'echo scripts/foo.ps1' / 'cat scripts/foo.sh' (path as a non-executed argument) falsely counted as a script invocation, letting -CoverageOnly pass without the workflow running the check. A gate matcher must anchor to the EXECUTED command (start-of-line or after an interpreter+flags), not match a token anywhere in the line.

The em-dash/en-dash ban (§3.14) has NO CI enforcement, so violations accumulate undetected: a repo census found 1442 em + 27 en dashes across 62 non-grandfathered files (incl. instruction/playbook/script files). Bot flagged em-dash literals in the new tolerance regex [-emdash] and test fixtures; correct form is a Unicode escape [-\u2014] (regex) or [char]0x2014 (test) so banned chars are matched/tested without embedding a literal. Root cause is missing machinery - a check-no-smart-punctuation CI gate (excluding the grandfathered catalog) would catch all of these mechanically.
divergence_override_history: prior batch purged some docs; ban is prose-only

finding_brief: Copilot bot caught a vacuous-truth exemption: check-commit-message.ps1 Get-MessageFindings exempted any subject starting with the Revert-quote prefix when ALL body lines matched the revert template, but an EMPTY body vacuously satisfies all-match, so a single-line Revert subject with a trailing period was fully exempt and bypassed trailing-period / subject-too-long. Fix: require at least one real This-reverts-commit-hex line (hasEvidence flag) before exempting.

Copilot bot caught a diagnostic-vs-behavior mismatch: run-local-ci.ps1 Test-MirrorCoverage rejects a tab ANYWHERE on a line but the message said tab in indentation is not supported, misleading triage for a tab inside a run string. Fix: message now states tabs are unsupported anywhere (column-based scan).

Copilot bot caught a fail-open exit code: check-sync-parity.ps1 exited ExitEnv (2, the run-local-ci SKIP code) when the pwsh sync GENERATOR itself failed, masking a real failure as an environment skip. Exit 2 must be reserved for the bash-side-cannot-run case. Fix: added ExitFail=3 (non-skippable) for the generator-failure path; contract doc updated; dogfood-probed (forced generator failure now exits 3 not 2).
Checker scripts defaulted RepoRoot to cwd, so invoking from a non-root directory scanned the wrong tree and could silently false-pass instead of resolving the repo root fail-closed
Repository checks tested .git as a directory, which breaks linked git worktrees where .git is a file; use git rev-parse --git-dir instead
A scanner with optional roots could scan zero items and pass; coverage scanners need an anti-vacuous minimum count/floor
Tests used a platform-specific path separator, so the intended nested path was not exercised on Linux
An unguarded git invocation threw CommandNotFoundException when git was absent instead of falling through to the scripted no-git path

Re-derive panel (duck-logic) caught a bootstrap fail-open the original an earlier PR panel had converged with: check-post-code-change.ps1 and check-comment-audit.ps1 blanket-skipped the first-ever add of the audit file (and the never-existed case), so a panel/audit-required introduction commit could pass CI with a missing/stale/invalid ledger if the local hook was bypassed. Fixed: removed both skips so every required commit is validated against its parent (the bootstrap commit ledger is present and validatable); both sibling gates hardened symmetrically with tests.

Re-derive panel (duck-logic) flagged that both sibling audit gates use git log --no-merges, so a merge commit carrying conflict-resolution or manual code/governance edits is never classified or ledger-validated. Downgraded to non-blocking given this repo strictly linear (rebase + force-with-lease) PR workflow. Follow-up: harden both gates together (fail closed on merge commits or validate against first-parent diffs).

Three review-pass-only slugs were appended at end-of-file and landed under the deterministic checker-scoped section, whose intro prose universally claims its members are registry-mechanized and not lens-forwarded; the review-pass-only rows violate that binding container-prose. The panel dismissed the placement as cosmetic (routing is per-row scope_mode) and missed the section-intro-vs-member-property contradiction. Bot-caught on an earlier PR; fixed by relocating the rows to the lower-frequency review-pass-only section.

NEW candidate (HIGH, ~9 comments across 5 consuming PRs): Compile-checked API shape after refactors. Proposed rule: Verify changed overload calls, component refs, callbacks, lambdas, and nullable expressions against the real API and language type rules before review.

NEW candidate (HIGH, ~6 comments across 1 consuming PR): Root-level ::deep in scoped Razor CSS leaks globally. Proposed rule: When scoped CSS uses ::deep, keep a component-owned ancestor before ::deep so the rule remains scoped to that component.

NEW candidate (HIGH, ~6 comments across 4 consuming PRs): Failure and timeout paths must preserve the true outcome. Proposed rule: When handling protocol mismatches, deserialization failures, safety breakers, timeout races, capped buffers, or cancellation, preserve the specific cause, terminal state, and originating token in the reported outcome.

NEW candidate (HIGH, ~4 comments across 1 consuming PR): Debug.Assert used as runtime validation. Proposed rule: Do not rely on Debug.Assert to validate external, unmanaged, or serialized input because Release builds must still fail fast on unexpected values.

NEW candidate (HIGH, ~4 comments across 3 consuming PRs): State identity uses representative rows, stale indexes, or partial keys. Proposed rule: When preserving focus, grouping, cache validity, or rank equivalence, use the stable domain identity and complete key fields rather than representative items, stale indexes, reference equality, or partial keys.

NEW candidate (HIGH, ~4 comments across 2 consuming PRs): User-action side effects cross the commit boundary. Proposed rule: Do not show progress, clear existing state, or honor cancellation side effects until the user has committed the operation and before the operation has crossed its irreversible commit boundary.

NEW candidate (MEDIUM, ~4 comments across 2 consuming PRs): Reusable component contracts drift from native semantics. Proposed rule: Reusable UI component parameters, ref collections, event callbacks, and event modifiers must model the native event args, nullable lifecycle, and subscription semantics they wrap.

NEW candidate (HIGH, ~3 comments across 1 consuming PR): Serialized discriminator rename breaks old payloads. Proposed rule: When a serialized type discriminator is renamed, readers must continue accepting legacy discriminator values or include a migration for persisted and exported payloads.

NEW candidate (HIGH, ~3 comments across 1 consuming PR): ARIA relationships point at unmounted targets. Proposed rule: ARIA relationship attributes such as aria-controls must reference a stable element that exists whenever the attribute is emitted.

NEW candidate (LOW, ~2 comments across 1 consuming PR): No-op progress sink allocated when null already disables progress. Proposed rule: If null is the established signal that a caller does not consume progress, do not allocate a no-op progress sink on a sibling path.

NEW candidate (HIGH, ~2 comments across 2 consuming PRs): RID, Platform, and native architecture signals disagree. Proposed rule: MSBuild RuntimeIdentifier, Platform, PlatformTarget, and native package selection must resolve to the same target architecture for every build and publish path.

NEW candidate (MEDIUM, ~2 comments across 1 consuming PR): Recursive input skipping can stack overflow. Proposed rule: Use loops rather than recursion to skip repeated ignorable input records from pipes, streams, parsers, or other untrusted sources.

NEW candidate (HIGH, ~2 comments across 2 consuming PRs): Parallel partial-load deltas lose source ordering. Proposed rule: When parallel work emits partial deltas that downstream state treats as ordered, restore the source ordering before dispatching each delta.

NEW candidate (MEDIUM, ~2 comments across 1 consuming PR): Unconditional event bindings with no handler. Proposed rule: Do not subscribe UI events that have no delegate unless an event modifier or other behavior requires the subscription.

NEW candidate (MEDIUM, ~2 comments across 1 consuming PR): DOM tests assert serialized HTML instead of semantics. Proposed rule: UI tests should assert semantic DOM state such as text content, attributes, and nodes instead of renderer-specific InnerHtml serialization.

NEW candidate (HIGH, ~2 comments across 2 consuming PRs): Tests seed impossible or inconsistent state shapes. Proposed rule: Test fixtures must preserve the same cross-field and parent-child invariants that production reducers, loaders, or factories create.

NEW candidate (HIGH, ~2 comments across 1 consuming PR): Machine-readable numeric formatting uses current culture. Proposed rule: Format values that will be parsed by compilers, query languages, protocols, or persisted schemas with invariant culture, not the current UI culture.

NEW candidate (HIGH, ~1 comment across 1 consuming PR): Raw delimiters in composite keys collide with data. Proposed rule: Composite string keys built from user or data values must encode lengths or escape delimiters so distinct tuples cannot collide.

NEW candidate (MEDIUM, ~1 comment across 1 consuming PR): Stale sentinel artifact guard only covers one state combination. Proposed rule: A stale backup, lock, marker, or sentinel artifact that blocks a retry must be checked independently of the primary artifact state.

NEW candidate (HIGH, ~1 comment across 1 consuming PR): Per-item keyboard actions are unreachable. Proposed rule: Interactive actions for individual chips, rows, or tokens must be reachable by keyboard, not only by pointer or a special last-item shortcut.

NEW candidate (MEDIUM, ~1 comment across 1 consuming PR): Render-time validator is invoked multiple times per value. Proposed rule: If a render path consults caller-provided validation more than once for the same value, compute it once and reuse the result for that render.

NEW candidate (MEDIUM, ~1 comment across 1 consuming PR): Normalization enforced in UI but not at persistence boundary. Proposed rule: Normalize and validate data again at persistence or import boundaries when UI-level normalization is not the only producer.

NEW candidate (HIGH, ~1 comment across 1 consuming PR): ARIA owned-role structure is invalid. Proposed rule: When assigning ARIA container roles, ensure every required direct child role is present and non-owned controls are wrapped or moved out.

NEW candidate (LOW, ~1 comment across 1 consuming PR): Theme colors bypass design tokens. Proposed rule: Component styles should derive colors from theme or design tokens rather than hard-coded color literals that can drift across themes.

NEW candidate (HIGH, ~1 comment across 1 consuming PR): Collision validation scope differs from allowed domain. Proposed rule: Name or uniqueness validation must check the same domain scope that the rest of the feature treats as mutually exclusive.

NEW candidate (LOW, ~1 comment across 1 consuming PR): Component ref cache keeps null captures. Proposed rule: Caches of component or element refs must prune entries whose captured ref has been cleared to null, not only entries whose domain item disappeared.

NEW candidate (MEDIUM, ~1 comment across 1 consuming PR): Expensive fallback is eager despite being rarely needed. Proposed rule: Defer expensive fallback loading or I/O until the later parser or formatter actually needs the fallback data.

NEW candidate (MEDIUM, ~1 comment across 1 consuming PR): Focus restoration misses removal of focused element. Proposed rule: When a rendered collection can remove the element that currently has focus, restore focus on every removal path, not only one mode-specific path.

NEW candidate (HIGH, ~1 comment across 1 consuming PR): Test double does not honor edge-case contract. Proposed rule: Fakes and test doubles must implement documented interface edge cases, especially terminal-state and idempotency semantics used by production code.

NEW candidate (LOW, ~1 comment across 1 consuming PR): Markdown placeholders parsed as HTML tags. Proposed rule: Markdown documentation should escape or code-format angle-bracket placeholders so renderers do not treat them as HTML tags.

NEW candidate (HIGH, ~1 comment across 1 consuming PR): Test DI collapses distinct production services into one singleton. Proposed rule: Tests that register dependency-injection fakes must preserve whether production injects distinct instances or a shared singleton.

NEW candidate (HIGH, ~1 comment across 1 consuming PR): Idempotent no-op action resets state. Proposed rule: When an action requests the state that is already active, reducers should preserve related user state instead of resetting direction, collapse, selection, or filters.

NEW candidate (HIGH, ~1 comment across 1 consuming PR): ICollection CopyTo writes before validating destination. Proposed rule: ICollection CopyTo implementations must validate index and capacity before copying so invalid calls do not partially mutate the destination.

NEW candidate (HIGH, ~1 comment across 1 consuming PR): Dictionary indexer hides duplicate domain keys. Proposed rule: When duplicate domain keys would corrupt lookup semantics, fail fast or disambiguate rather than assigning through a dictionary indexer that silently overwrites.

NEW candidate (HIGH, ~1 comment across 1 consuming PR): Persisted ordering can contain duplicates. Proposed rule: Before using persisted user ordering to build unique headers or columns, de-duplicate while preserving first occurrence.

NEW candidate (HIGH, ~1 comment across 1 consuming PR): CSV formula neutralization ignores leading spaces. Proposed rule: CSV and spreadsheet formula neutralization must inspect the first non-whitespace character, not only the first raw character.

NEW candidate (HIGH, ~1 comment across 1 consuming PR): Handled listbox keys still scroll the browser. Proposed rule: When custom keyboard navigation handles arrow, Home, or End keys, prevent the browser default for those keys while preserving Tab.

NEW candidate (LOW, ~1 comment across 1 consuming PR): Icon and label render without separator. Proposed rule: Reusable components that render an icon next to text must insert explicit spacing so labels do not visually run into icons.

NEW candidate (HIGH, ~1 comment across 1 consuming PR): CI does not exercise the claimed build path. Proposed rule: If a PR claims CI validates a specialized build path, the workflow must set the properties or commands that actually execute that path.

NEW candidate (HIGH, ~1 comment across 1 consuming PR): OS-specific batch-size limit ignored. Proposed rule: When a platform API documents lower limits on supported OS versions, choose batch sizes by OS capability or retry with a safe fallback.

MINT from the doc-impl recurrence class: a user-facing/log/exception string asserts an outcome a later branch can cancel, suppress, defer, or reinterpret (3 misses across consuming PRs); carved into a focused rule rather than bloating doc-impl-mismatch
Intentional narrowing/reinterpret numeric cast written as plain (int)x instead of unchecked((int)x); external bot caught, pre-PR multi-model panel missed
Fail-closed parser matched recognized tokens case-sensitively, silently misclassifying a valid mis-cased token (parity/collision hazard); external bot caught, pre-PR panel missed
Structured-text (XML/JSON/SQL) construction escaped only some injected values, leaving a sibling injected raw (validity/injection + missing read-side decode); external bot caught, pre-PR panel missed

Fail-closed parser returned its canonical (parsed) node when it captured no non-empty identifying field, collapsing distinct non-canonical inputs to one all-empty node (hash/dedup collision); external bot caught, pre-PR panel missed

Equality/comparison on a hot path serialized both operands to throwaway buffers (2 ArrayBufferWriter + full encode per call) instead of streaming; external bot caught (perf), pre-PR panel missed; note: streaming rewrite must stay byte-exact with the hash encoding or merge/hash drift

Playbook (comment-hygiene-purge.md:14) grounded an ask_user-skip exemption in comment-protocol.md's 'deletion' exempt category, but the cited canonical (comment-protocol.md:52 + the playbook's own line 101) attests purge removals as a ledger-row COUNT, not a deletion - a claim mischaracterizing its cited canonical doc. doc-impl-mismatch covered doc-vs-code only; NO rule owned doc-vs-cited-canonical-doc: rule absent for that class -> panel-miss. Remediated by adding a cited-canonical-doc sub-case to doc-impl-mismatch.

Ledger token 'ran, 0 new-or-rewritten' fused a count into the status-only comment-audit-S3.1 row (canonical grammar is status-only) at 4 sibling sites - a divergent restated mirror of the canonical row. mirrored-representation-parity's original trigger+procedure already reached this (a mirror diverging from its canonical), but at LOW tier it was forwarded+applicable yet mis-dispositioned by the originating full-mode panel: rule existed and applied -> panel-execution-failure. Remediated by LOW->HIGH bump (force-acked per-commit).

Worked KV-v1 LEDGER example (post-code-change.md) omitted grammar-mandated fields (profile= on core, difit= on gates) - a field-set-parity gap between a mirror and its canonical. The original mirrored-representation-parity trigger keyed on 'changes a structure', NOT covering a standing/added mirror whose canonical is unchanged in the diff: rule absent for that trigger shape -> panel-miss (same shape as the sibling doc-impl-mismatch cited-canonical-doc miss: existing slug, sub-case added). Remediated by adding an added-mirror trigger clause + LOW->HIGH bump.

Publish-gate-receipt schema doc instructed computing the receipt remote identity from the FETCH URL (git remote get-url <remote>) while the enforcing mechanism binds the PUSH URL (pre-push hook $2, i.e. git remote get-url --push); a repo with a distinct remote.<name>.pushurl would produce a receipt that never matches -> false-block. The design + diff multi-model panels reviewed the schema doc but did not cross-check the compute-instruction's URL variant against the checker's $2 binding. Class: a doc compute-instruction sources an identity from the wrong URL variant vs the runtime mechanism (sibling of the docs-mirror-runtime / API-name-mirrors-code checks). Remediated in-PR: doc now says --push + explains why; doc-impl-mismatch strengthened with a Compute-instruction-source-variant sub-case.

A carry-forward review-response edit re-wrote panel-misses.csv with PowerShell Set-Content -Encoding ascii, lossy-converting the existing rows' section-sign and arrow characters to '?' - an encoding-loss invisible to the smart-punctuation gate (which bans ADDED banned chars, not LOST non-ASCII) that slipped past the carry-forward review-response (no re-panel). Class: a lossy ascii encoding conversion silently drops non-ASCII (section signs / unicode / arrows) from a file that already had them; no deterministic gate flags a non-ASCII-count DECREASE in an edited file. Caught by the Copilot PR reviewer. Remediated in-PR: restored the file from base as UTF-8, kept the appended row.

Agent emitted the PRE-COMMIT GATE PASSED block (incl. the ~55-slug core_rules_acknowledged) in the turn AFTER the git commit tool call instead of before it, and wrote a 'see PRE-COMMIT block this turn' forward-reference in the panel block - a s1B block-ordering violation. Every underlying gate genuinely passed and the block content was truthful (a late/mis-ordered emission, self-caught + self-reported before any push, not a falsified attestation). Root cause: s1B block emission was behavioral-only with no fail-closed receipt backstop, unlike the sibling POST-CODE-CHANGE LEDGER and comment-audit receipts. Remediated by mechanizing a 4th audit receipt (check-pre-commit-gate.ps1: pre-commit hook receipt + pre-push copilot-audit-precommit note gate) and banning the forward-reference placeholder.

Whole-branch publish-gate panel (5 reviewers, 2 rounds) plus deterministic gates missed a nested-loop accumulation-granularity bug: a list accumulated the parent container identifier once per inner child, producing duplicate entries that inflate a downstream .Count-based summary; correct granularity was once per affected parent. Caught by the Copilot PR reviewer, not any local scan. No existing catalog slug targets accumulator-adds-parent-id-per-child-in-nested-loop-inflating-counts. Remediated in-PR: flag + single add after the loop, strengthened exact-equality test + new survive-path test. Consuming-project PR; user declined to file a governance issue.

Copilot PR reviewer caught a structured-block validator omitting a schema-declared field (a diff-shown flag) and accepting a whitespace-only value on a presence check (a subject header) - the pre-PR multi-model diff panel verified 'presence-first on every load-bearing key' at a high level but did NOT enumerate every field the block schema declares to confirm each is validated, nor test the empty/whitespace boundary of each presence regex (the regex .+? matches a lone space).

Copilot PR reviewer caught a mode-specific error hint pointing at the wrong parent (current tip vs first-parent under an amend flag) and a missing unit test for the amend accept-branch - the diff panel flagged the sibling doc's amend parent rule but did not cross-check the checker's own error-hint text for the same mode, nor notice the amend acceptance path had no test.
